LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, JUN-30-2020 HEARING HELD RE: CROSS-DEFENDANTS FFG RESTAURANT GROUP, INC. AND FORWARD FOOD GROUP, LLC'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: FFG RESTAURANT GROUP, INC. AND FORWARD FOOD GROUP, LLC'S MOTION TO STRIKE IS DENIED. THE ADDUCED ALLEGATIONS REGARDING LITZ'S MENACING CONDUCT ARE NOT FALSE, IRRELEVANT OR IMPROPER WITHIN THE MEANING OF CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE 436 AND THEY SUPPORT THE ASSAULT AND INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS CLAIMS. CROSS-COMPLAINANTS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR# 6185, TONYVAUGHN@YAHOO.COM (302/EPS)

Off Calendar Jun-15-2020 Continued To Aug-12-20 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-30-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA COURTCALL): Atty. LUCINDA L. STORM, Esq. of Law Offices of Lucina L. Storm (Tel: 415-777-6990) for Cross-Complainants Laura Ozyilmaz And Sayat Ozyilmaz;
Atty. DAVID H. SCHWARTZ, Esq. if Law Offices of David H. Schwartz (Tel: 415-399-9301) for Cross-Defendants John Litz and Yongjia Sollers.

HEARING RE: CROSS-DEFENDANTS FFG RESTAURANT GROUP, INC. AND FORWARD FOOD GROUP, LLC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT.

No appearance by counsel for movants. Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling:

The demurrers to the first cause of action for wrongful termination, eighth cause of action for breach of contract, and eighteenth cause of action for breach of the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing are overruled.

The demurrers to the wrongful termination and retaliation claims (causes of action two through five) are overruled.

The demurrers to the promissory fraud and fraud in the inducement claims (causes of action six through seven) are overruled.

The demurrer to cause of action nine for breach of the participation agreements is overruled.
The demurrer to the eleventh cause of action for assault is overruled.

FFG Restaurant Group, Inc. and Forward Food Group, LLC's demurrer to the twelfth cause of action for defamation is sustained with ten days leave to amend.

The demurrer to the thirteenth cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress is overruled.

The demurrers to causes of action fifteen through seventeen (wage claims) are overruled.

The demurrer to the nineteenth cause of action for unjust enrichment is sustained without leave to amend.

The demurrer to the twentieth cause of action for intentional interference with prospective advantage is overruled.

The demurrer to the twenty-first cause of action for violation of the UCL is overruled.

(See Word minutes for complete ruling.)

Movants to prepare and submit a proposed order.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR# 6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com (302/EPS)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-30-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA COURTCALL): Atty. LUCINDA L. STORM, Esq. of Law Offices of Lucina L. Storm (Tel: 415-777-6990) for Cross-Complainants Laura Ozyilmaz And Sayat Ozyilmaz;
Atty. DAVID H. SCHWARTZ, Esq. if Law Offices of David H. Schwartz (Tel: 415-399-9301) for Cross-Defendants John Litz and Yongjia Sollers.

HEARING RE: CROSS-DEFENDANTS JOHN LITZ AND YONGJIA SOLLERS' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling:

The demurrers are overruled as to causes of action one through five, nine, and fifteen. As noted in the companion demurrer, cross-complainants sufficiently allege alter ego liability against Litz and the cited agreements are sufficiently alleged.

The demurrer to the tenth cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty is overruled. Litz fails to demonstrate that the claim needs to be brought as a derivative action. Paragraphs 169-179 allege that Litz is the managing member of the LLC and that he has been abusing that power to cross-complainants' detriment. (See Feresi v. The Livery, LLV (2014) 232 Cal.App.4th 414, 425 ["The manager of an LLC has a fiduciary duty and owes to the members of the LLC the same duties of loyalty and good faith as a partner owes to the partnership and its partners."].)

The demurrer to the twelfth cause of action for defamation is sustained with ten days leave to amend as to Litz and overruled as to Sollers. Paragraphs 192 - 197 sufficiently allege Sollers' defamatory statements. The Ozyilmazs' have leave to amend to allege facts showing how Litz is liable for his wife's comments and/or allege the specific defamatory statements that Litz uttered.

The court rejects Litz and Sollers' attempt to incorporate by reference the co-cross-defendants' demurrers and arguments.

Movant to prepare and submit a proposed order.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR# 6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com (302/EPS)

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-30-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA COURTCALL): Atty. LUCINDA L. STORM, Esq. of Law Offices of Lucina L. Storm (Tel: 415-777-6990) for Cross-Complainants Laura Ozyilmaz And Sayat Ozyilmaz;
Atty. DAVID H. SCHWARTZ, Esq. if Law Offices of David H. Schwartz (Tel: 415-399-9301) for Cross-Defendants John Litz and Yongjia Sollers.

HEARING RE: CROSS-DEFENDANTS FFG RESTAURANT GROUP, INC. AND FORWARD FOOD GROUP, LLC'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT.

No appearance by counsel for movants. Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling:

FFG Restaurant Group, Inc. and Forward Food Group, LLC's motion to strike is denied. The adduced allegations regarding Litz's menacing conduct are not false, irrelevant or improper within the meaning of Code of Civil Procedure 436 and they support the assault and intentional infliction of emotional distress claims.

Cross-Complainants to prepare and submit a proposed order.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR# 6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com (302/EPS)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)

Defendant And Cross-Complainant Laura Ozyilmaz And Sayat Ozyilmaz?S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Mediationarbitration And To Stay Claims
Off Calendar Jun-23-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: Defendant And Cross-Complainant Laura Ozyilmaz And Sayat Ozyilmaz's Motion To Compel Mediation/Arbitration And To Stay Claims.

No appearances made by the parties. The Court adopts its tentative ruling:

Defendants and cross-complainants Ozyilmazs' motion to compel "mediation/arbitration" is denied.

Defendants' motion to compel mediation is denied. There is no recognized authority allowing such relief as mediation is voluntary and a party may withdraw at anytime. (See Gaines v. Fidelity National Title Ins. Co. (2016) 62 Cal. 4th 1081, 1103; Jeld-Wen, Inc. v. Superior Court (2007) 146 Cal.App.4th 536, 541.)

Defendants' motion to compel arbitration is denied. Defendants bear the burden of proving the existence of a valid arbitration agreement by a preponderance of the evidence. (Pinnacle Museum Tower Assn. v, Pinnacle Market Development (US), LLC (2012) 55 Cal.4th 223, 236). In this case, defendants fail to demonstrate the existence of an agreement to arbitrate. Section 14 of the adduced Memorandum of Understanding ("MOU") provides the following:

If the parties are unable to resolve a material matter, they agree to submit to a full day of mediation or such lesser time as necessary to resolve the matter, to be administered by the American Arbitration Association under its Commercial Mediation Rules, within five (5) business days following any party's request therefor. (Storm Dec., Ex. A.)

The MOU says nothing about arbitration. Recognizing this deficiency, defendants pivot to the arbitration agreement at section 25.13 of the unexecuted Shareholders' Agreement. Mr. Litz and the entity plaintiffs (FFG Restaurant Group, Inc. Forward Food Group, LLC) are not bound to arbitrate under the Shareholders' Agreement because they never agreed to its terms. "Because arbitration is a contractual matter, a party who has not agreed to arbitrate a controversy cannot be compelled to do so." (Harris v. Tap Worldwide, LLC (2016) 248 Cal.App.4th 373, 380.) The fact that Mr. Litz and defendants are shareholders in FFG Restaurant Group, Inc. is irrelevant. They became shareholders before the existence of the MOU and proposed Shareholders' Agreement. There is no evidence that they subsequently agreed to arbitrate their disputes.

Counsel for the plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)
